content.text:
Subject: English
Term: III
Grade: 6
Lesson: The Jungle Book by Rudyard Kipling.

Summary: 'The Jungle Book' is a classic story loved by audiences of all ages. It primarily deals with the early childhood of a young boy named Mowgli, who is raised in the midst of wild animals in the Indian forest. The plot revolves around his survival, acceptance into the wolf pack, and his future destiny.

1. Father Wolf: The chief of the wolves, protective and strictly follows the Law of the Jungle.
2. Mother Wolf (Raksha / The Demon): A fierce protector with a grey nose who mothers four cubs. She courageously adopts the Man's cub.
3. Tabaqui (The Jackal): A cunning, mischievous scavenger who acts as a messenger and feeds on scraps like dry bones.
4. Shere Khan (The Tiger): The antagonist. Known as the Big One from Waingunga River. He is lame in one foot (nicknamed 'Lungri') and hunts cattle and men.
5. Man's Cub (Mowgli): A bold, naked human baby who wanders into the wolves' cave.
6. Narrator: Describes the events of the jungle.

Scene I: Set in the Seeonee Hills at 7 PM. Father Wolf wakes up. Tabaqui visits, eats a dry bone, and maliciously informs the wolves that Shere Khan has shifted his hunting grounds to their hills. Father Wolf is angry because Shere Khan's presence will scare away the game.

Scene II: The wolves hear Shere Khan hunting. To their shock, he is not hunting animals, but Man. The purr of the tiger creates panic.

Scene III: The Law of the Jungle forbids eating Man because it brings destructive humans with guns to the forest. Shere Khan jumps at a woodcutter's campfire and burns his feet. Fleeing the fire, a naked human baby wanders into the wolves' cave. Father Wolf gently brings it in. Shere Khan arrives and demands his 'quarry'. Mother Wolf fiercely defends the baby, claiming he will live to hunt Shere Khan. Shere Khan retreats.

Scene IV: Mother Wolf decides to keep the baby. She names him 'Mowgli the Frog' because he is hairless. She prophesies that he will eventually hunt Shere Khan. The play hints at his future adventures with Baloo and Bagheera.

sections.title: 4. The Law of the Jungle (Important Concept)

content.text:
The Law of the Jungle forbids any beast to eat Man. 
Reason 1 (Real Reason): Man-killing brings men on elephants with guns, rockets, and torches, making the whole jungle suffer.
Reason 2 (Beasts' Reason): Man is the weakest and most defenceless of all living things. Hunting him is beneath their dignity.
Consequence: It is believed that Man-eaters become mangy and lose their teeth.

sections.title: 5. Book Back Questions - Reading (One Mark Concepts)

content.text:
Q: What human characteristics do the animals exhibit?
A: They converse, follow laws, hold grudges, express anger, and show motherly affection.

Q: Did Tabaqui receive a warm welcome?
A: No, Father Wolf spoke in a stiff tone, showing they disliked his mischievous nature.

Q: Tabaqui acts as a ______ to the pack of wolves.
A: messenger.

Q: Who is about to go on a hunt? Do the wolves panic on his arrival?
A: Father Wolf is preparing to hunt. They do not panic, but they get angry when Shere Khan arrives.

sections.title: 6. Book Back Questions - Match the Following

content.text:
1. Father Wolf -> the chief of the Wolves
2. Tabaqui -> begs for meat and thanks for the meal/warns the wolves about Shere Khan
3. Mother Wolf -> with a grey nose... feeds her four cubs
4. Shere Khan -> the big one from Waingunga River with a lame foot

Sentences are classified into:
1. Declarative (D) - Statements.
2. Interrogative (In) - Questions.
3. Exclamatory (E) - Expresses strong feelings (!).
4. Imperative (Im) - Commands or requests.

Examples from Book Back:
- Vendor: How many kilos do you want? (Interrogative)
- Tourist at Taj Mahal: What a beautiful monument! (Exclamatory)
- Teacher noticing students talking: Keep quiet! (Imperative)
- Student introducing oneself: My name is Ram. (Declarative)

Articles (a, an, the) are used to modify nouns.
Textbook Exercises:
1. Sujatha is reading AN interesting story in the library.
2. Vishal drew A wonderful picture on the board yesterday.
3. Srinath and Sandhya were bored at AN awful concert.
4. Purvaja ate A salad of raw vegetables for dinner.
5. Suresh was astonished to see A huge statue.
6. The child was playing with A yellow balloon.

Adjectives describe nouns.
Textbook Dialogue Fill-ups:
Venkat was the FIRST one to enroll.
He enrolled in a FEW events.
He gave his name for the HIGH jump.
Sinduja decided to participate in ALL the events.
(Adjectives used: first, few, high, all, best, etc.)

"Each dog barks in his own yard!" - Shere Khan's dialogue when retreating.
"For so mean a person as myself a dry bone is a good feast." - Tabaqui.
"The Wolves are a free people. They take orders from the Head of the Pack." - Father Wolf.

questions.q: Who is the author of 'The Jungle Book'? || 'The Jungle Book' (ஜங்கிள் புக்) ஆசிரியர் யார்?
options: R.K. Narayan || R.K. Narayan
Rudyard Kipling || Rudyard Kipling
Ruskin Bond || Ruskin Bond
Enid Blyton || Enid Blyton
questions.ex: The Jungle Book was written by Rudyard Kipling. It is a famous story that deals with the early childhood of a boy among wild animals. || The Jungle Book was written by Rudyard Kipling. It is a famous story that deals with the early childhood of a boy among wild animals.

questions.q: Where does the opening scene of the play take place? || நாடகத்தின் ஆரம்பக் காட்சி எங்கு நடைபெறுகிறது?
options: Waingunga River || Waingunga River
Seeonee Hills || Seeonee Hills
Himalayan Mountains || Himalayan Mountains
Thar Desert || Thar Desert
questions.ex: The scene opens in the Seeonee Hills at seven o'clock on a sunny evening in the middle of the jungle. || The scene opens in the Seeonee Hills at seven o'clock on a sunny evening in the middle of the jungle.

questions.q: What is the name of the Jackal in the story? || கதையில் வரும் குள்ளநரியின் பெயர் என்ன?
options: Baloo || Baloo
Bagheera || Bagheera
Shere Khan || Shere Khan
Tabaqui || Tabaqui
questions.ex: Tabaqui is the Jackal who visits Father and Mother Wolf to cause mischief and deliver news about Shere Khan. || Tabaqui is the Jackal who visits Father and Mother Wolf to cause mischief and deliver news about Shere Khan.

questions.q: What did Tabaqui eat at the entrance of the Wolf's cave? || ஓநாயின் குகை நுழைவாயிலில் தபாகி என்ன சாப்பிட்டது?
options: Fresh meat || Fresh meat
A dry bone || A dry bone
Fruits || Fruits
Nothing || Nothing
questions.ex: Tabaqui states that for a mean person like himself, a dry bone is a good feast, and he eats it while looking at the wolf cubs. || Tabaqui states that for a mean person like himself, a dry bone is a good feast, and he eats it while looking at the wolf cubs.

questions.q: Who had shifted his hunting grounds to the Seeonee Hills? || தனது வேட்டைத் தளத்தை சியோனி மலைகளுக்கு மாற்றியது யார்?
options: Tabaqui || Tabaqui
Shere Khan || Shere Khan
Father Wolf || Father Wolf
Lungri the bear || Lungri the bear
questions.ex: Tabaqui informs the wolves that Shere Khan, the Big one, has shifted his hunting grounds to the hills for the next moon. || Tabaqui informs the wolves that Shere Khan, the Big one, has shifted his hunting grounds to the hills for the next moon.

questions.q: Where did Shere Khan usually live before coming to the Seeonee Hills? || சியோனி மலைகளுக்கு வருவதற்கு முன்பு ஷேர்கான் வழக்கமாக எங்கு வசித்தது?
options: In the village || In the village
Near the Waingunga River || Near the Waingunga River
In a dry cave || In a dry cave
On a tall mountain || On a tall mountain
questions.ex: Shere Khan was the tiger who lived near the Waingunga River, twenty miles away. || Shere Khan was the tiger who lived near the Waingunga River, twenty miles away.

questions.q: What nickname did Shere Khan's mother give him because he was lame in one foot? || ஷேர்கான் ஒரு காலில் முடமாக இருந்ததால் அவனது தாய் அவனுக்கு என்ன செல்லப்பெயர் சூட்டினார்?
options: Lungri || Lungri
Raksha || Raksha
Demon || Demon
Frog || Frog
questions.ex: His mother called him 'Lungri' (the Lame One) because he had been lame in one foot from birth. || His mother called him 'Lungri' (the Lame One) because he had been lame in one foot from birth.

questions.q: Why did Shere Khan hunt only cattle? || ஷேர்கான் ஏன் கால்நடைகளை மட்டும் வேட்டையாடியது?
options: He loved the taste of cattle || He loved the taste of cattle
He was lame in one foot and couldn't hunt fast animals || He was lame in one foot and couldn't hunt fast animals
The law of the jungle told him to || The law of the jungle told him to
He was afraid of wild bucks || He was afraid of wild bucks
questions.ex: Because he was lame in one foot from birth, he wasn't fast enough to hunt wild game, so he only killed cattle. || Because he was lame in one foot from birth, he wasn't fast enough to hunt wild game, so he only killed cattle.

questions.q: According to the Law of the Jungle, what is forbidden for beasts to eat? || காட்டு விதிகளின்படி, விலங்குகள் எதைச் சாப்பிடுவது தடைசெய்யப்பட்டுள்ளது?
options: Insects || Insects
Cattle || Cattle
Man || Man
Dry bones || Dry bones
questions.ex: The Law of the Jungle forbids every beast to eat Man, except when teaching their children how to kill outside their hunting grounds. || The Law of the Jungle forbids every beast to eat Man, except when teaching their children how to kill outside their hunting grounds.

questions.q: What happens if a beast kills a Man, according to the real reason in the Law of the Jungle? || காட்டு விதியின் உண்மையான காரணத்தின்படி, ஒரு விலங்கு ஒரு மனிதனைக் கொன்றால் என்ன நடக்கும்?
options: They get a reward || They get a reward
It brings men on elephants with guns and torches || It brings men on elephants with guns and torches
The beast becomes the king of the jungle || The beast becomes the king of the jungle
Other animals start respecting the beast || Other animals start respecting the beast
questions.ex: Man-killing means the arrival of angry men on elephants with guns and rockets, causing everyone in the jungle to suffer. || Man-killing means the arrival of angry men on elephants with guns and rockets, causing everyone in the jungle to suffer.

questions.q: What is the reason beasts give among themselves for not hunting Man? || மனிதர்களை வேட்டையாடாமல் இருப்பதற்கு விலங்குகள் தங்களுக்குள் கூறும் காரணம் என்ன?
options: Man is too fast || Man is too fast
Man is the weakest and most defenceless living thing || Man is the weakest and most defenceless living thing
Man tastes bad || Man tastes bad
Man is poisonous || Man is poisonous
questions.ex: Beasts tell themselves that Man is the weakest and most defenceless of all living things, and it is beneath their dignity to hunt him. || Beasts tell themselves that Man is the weakest and most defenceless of all living things, and it is beneath their dignity to hunt him.

questions.q: What physical consequence happens to Man-eaters according to the jungle belief? || காட்டு நம்பிக்கையின்படி, மனிதர்களை உண்பவர்களுக்கு என்ன உடல்ரீதியான விளைவு ஏற்படும்?
options: They become mangy and lose their teeth || They become mangy and lose their teeth
They become very strong || They become very strong
They grow larger claws || They grow larger claws
They lose their vision || They lose their vision
questions.ex: It is believed that Man-eaters become mangy (having a skin disease) and lose their teeth. || It is believed that Man-eaters become mangy (having a skin disease) and lose their teeth.

questions.q: How did Shere Khan burn his feet? || ஷேர்கான் தனது பாதங்களை எவ்வாறு சுட்டுக்கொண்டது?
options: Walking on hot sand || Walking on hot sand
Fighting with Father Wolf || Fighting with Father Wolf
Jumping at a woodcutter's campfire || Jumping at a woodcutter's campfire
Falling into a volcano || Falling into a volcano
questions.ex: Father Wolf realizes that Shere Khan missed his prey because he foolishly jumped at a woodcutter's campfire and burned his feet. || Father Wolf realizes that Shere Khan missed his prey because he foolishly jumped at a woodcutter's campfire and burned his feet.

questions.q: What did Father Wolf see when he checked his mid-spring jump? || தந்தை ஓநாய் பாயும்போது எதைக் கண்டது?
options: A large bear || A large bear
A woodcutter || A woodcutter
A naked baby (Man's cub) || A naked baby (Man's cub)
A young deer || A young deer
questions.ex: Father Wolf jumped but stopped himself mid-air when he saw a naked Man's cub who could just walk. || Father Wolf jumped but stopped himself mid-air when he saw a naked Man's cub who could just walk.

questions.q: How did the Man's cub react when he saw Father Wolf? || தந்தை ஓநாயைக் கண்டதும் மனிதக் குட்டி எவ்வாறு நடந்துகொண்டது?
options: He started crying loudly || He started crying loudly
He ran away into the bushes || He ran away into the bushes
He picked up a stone || He picked up a stone
He looked up into Father Wolf's face and laughed || He looked up into Father Wolf's face and laughed
questions.ex: The baby was completely fearless. He looked right up into Father Wolf's face and laughed. || The baby was completely fearless. He looked right up into Father Wolf's face and laughed.

questions.q: When Father Wolf carried the baby to the cave, did his teeth hurt the baby? || தந்தை ஓநாய் குழந்தையைக் குகைக்குச் சுமந்து சென்றபோது, அதன் பற்கள் குழந்தையைக் காயப்படுத்தினவா?
options: Yes, they scratched his back || Yes, they scratched his back
No, not a tooth even scratched the skin || No, not a tooth even scratched the skin
Yes, he carried him by the hair || Yes, he carried him by the hair
The text does not mention this || The text does not mention this
questions.ex: A wolf can carry an egg without breaking it. Father Wolf's jaws closed on the child's back, but not a single tooth scratched his skin. || A wolf can carry an egg without breaking it. Father Wolf's jaws closed on the child's back, but not a single tooth scratched his skin.

questions.q: What did the Man's cub do once he was laid down among the wolf cubs? || ஓநாய்க் குட்டிகளுக்கு நடுவே படுக்க வைக்கப்பட்டதும் மனிதக் குட்டி என்ன செய்தது?
options: He crawled out of the cave || He crawled out of the cave
He pushed his way between the cubs to get close to the warm hide || He pushed his way between the cubs to get close to the warm hide
He started playing with a dry bone || He started playing with a dry bone
He fell asleep immediately || He fell asleep immediately
questions.ex: The baby pushed his way between the wolf cubs to get close to the mother's warm hide, showing no fear. || The baby pushed his way between the wolf cubs to get close to the mother's warm hide, showing no fear.

questions.q: Who followed the Man's cub to the mouth of the Wolf's cave? || மனிதக் குட்டியைப் பின்தொடர்ந்து ஓநாய் குகையின் வாயிலுக்கு வந்தது யார்?
options: Baloo the bear || Baloo the bear
Shere Khan and Tabaqui || Shere Khan and Tabaqui
The woodcutter || The woodcutter
A herd of cattle || A herd of cattle
questions.ex: Shere Khan's great square head blocked the moonlight at the cave's entrance, and Tabaqui was squeaking behind him. || Shere Khan's great square head blocked the moonlight at the cave's entrance, and Tabaqui was squeaking behind him.

questions.q: Why couldn't Shere Khan enter the cave to take the Man's cub? || மனிதக் குட்டியை எடுக்க ஷேர்கானால் ஏன் குகைக்குள் நுழைய முடியவில்லை?
options: He was afraid of the dark || He was afraid of the dark
The mouth of the cave was too narrow for him || The mouth of the cave was too narrow for him
Tabaqui stopped him || Tabaqui stopped him
He changed his mind || He changed his mind
questions.ex: Father Wolf knew the cave entrance was too narrow for a tiger. Shere Khan's shoulders and forepaws were cramped for space. || Father Wolf knew the cave entrance was too narrow for a tiger. Shere Khan's shoulders and forepaws were cramped for space.

questions.q: How did Father Wolf reply to Shere Khan's demand for the cub? || குட்டியைக் கேட்ட ஷேர்கானுக்கு தந்தை ஓநாய் எவ்வாறு பதிலளித்தது?
options: He happily gave the cub away || He happily gave the cub away
He said the Wolves are a free people and take orders only from the Head of the Pack || He said the Wolves are a free people and take orders only from the Head of the Pack
He offered Shere Khan a bullock instead || He offered Shere Khan a bullock instead
He told him to ask Mother Wolf || He told him to ask Mother Wolf
questions.ex: Father Wolf boldly stated that the Wolves are free people and do not take orders from any striped cattle-killer like Shere Khan. || Father Wolf boldly stated that the Wolves are free people and do not take orders from any striped cattle-killer like Shere Khan.

questions.q: What is Mother Wolf's other name, earned during her time fighting in the pack? || கூட்டத்தில் சண்டையிட்ட காலத்தில் தாய் ஓநாய் பெற்ற வேறு பெயர் என்ன?
options: Lungri || Lungri
Raksha, 'The Demon' || Raksha, 'The Demon'
The Chief || The Chief
Seeonee || Seeonee
questions.ex: Mother Wolf is called Raksha, which means 'The Demon', a name she won in fair fights. || Mother Wolf is called Raksha, which means 'The Demon', a name she won in fair fights.

questions.q: What did Mother Wolf say the Man's cub would eventually do to Shere Khan? || மனிதக் குட்டி இறுதியில் ஷேர்கானுக்கு என்ன செய்யும் என்று தாய் ஓநாய் கூறியது?
options: He would run away from him || He would run away from him
He would build a campfire to scare him || He would build a campfire to scare him
He would hunt Shere Khan || He would hunt Shere Khan
He would tame him || He would tame him
questions.ex: Mother Wolf fiercely told Shere Khan that the Man's cub would live to run with the pack, hunt with the pack, and in the end, he would hunt Shere Khan. || Mother Wolf fiercely told Shere Khan that the Man's cub would live to run with the pack, hunt with the pack, and in the end, he would hunt Shere Khan.

questions.q: Why did Shere Khan back down from the fight with Mother Wolf? || தாய் ஓநாயுடனான சண்டையிலிருந்து ஷேர்கான் ஏன் பின்வாங்கியது?
options: He was too tired to fight || He was too tired to fight
She had the advantage of the ground and would fight to the death || She had the advantage of the ground and would fight to the death
He heard the villagers coming || He heard the villagers coming
Tabaqui told him to retreat || Tabaqui told him to retreat
questions.ex: Shere Khan knew he couldn't stand against Mother Wolf inside the cave because she had the positional advantage and would fiercely fight to the death. || Shere Khan knew he couldn't stand against Mother Wolf inside the cave because she had the positional advantage and would fiercely fight to the death.

questions.q: What did Shere Khan say while retreating from the cave? || குகையிலிருந்து பின்வாங்கும்போது ஷேர்கான் என்ன கூறியது?
options: I will never return! || I will never return!
The man-cub is mine and will come to me in the end! || The man-cub is mine and will come to me in the end!
You can keep the boy. || You can keep the boy.
I am going back to Waingunga. || I am going back to Waingunga.
questions.ex: Shere Khan shouted, 'Each dog barks in his own yard! ... The man-cub is mine and will come to me in the end!' || Shere Khan shouted, 'Each dog barks in his own yard! ... The man-cub is mine and will come to me in the end!'

questions.q: What name did Mother Wolf give to the Man's cub? || மனிதக் குட்டிக்கு தாய் ஓநாய் என்ன பெயர் சூட்டியது?
options: Nathoo || Nathoo
Mowgli the Frog || Mowgli the Frog
Little Wolf || Little Wolf
Raksha || Raksha
questions.ex: Mother Wolf named him Mowgli the Frog because he was hairless like a frog when he came to them. || Mother Wolf named him Mowgli the Frog because he was hairless like a frog when he came to them.

questions.q: Who is described as 'the chief of the Wolves' in the matching exercise? || பொருத்துகப் பயிற்சியில் 'ஓநாய்களின் தலைவர்' என்று யார் விவரிக்கப்படுகிறார்?
options: Shere Khan || Shere Khan
Tabaqui || Tabaqui
Mother Wolf || Mother Wolf
Father Wolf || Father Wolf
questions.ex: In the book's matching exercise, Father Wolf is identified as the chief of the Wolves. || In the book's matching exercise, Father Wolf is identified as the chief of the Wolves.

questions.q: Tabaqui acts as a ____________ to the pack of wolves. || ஓநாய்க் கூட்டத்திற்கு தபாகி ஒரு ____________ ஆகச் செயல்படுகிறது.
options: guard || guard
messenger || messenger
friend || friend
enemy || enemy
questions.ex: Tabaqui acts as a messenger, bringing news about Shere Khan shifting his hunting grounds. || Tabaqui acts as a messenger, bringing news about Shere Khan shifting his hunting grounds.

questions.q: True or False: Mother Wolf was frightened by Shere Khan's roar and gave up the baby. || சரியா தவறா: ஷேர்கானின் கர்ஜனையைக் கேட்டு பயந்த தாய் ஓநாய் குழந்தையை விட்டுக்கொடுத்தது.
options: True || True
False || False
Cannot be determined || Cannot be determined
Partially True || Partially True
questions.ex: False. Mother Wolf shook herself clear of the cubs, sprang forward with blazing eyes, and fiercely defended the baby against Shere Khan. || False. Mother Wolf shook herself clear of the cubs, sprang forward with blazing eyes, and fiercely defended the baby against Shere Khan.

questions.q:
Assertion (A): The villagers of Waingunga were angry with Shere Khan.
Reason (R): Shere Khan had only killed cattle because of his lame foot. || கூற்று (A): வைய்ன்கங்கா கிராம மக்கள் ஷேர்கான் மீது கோபமாக இருந்தனர். காரணம் (R): ஷேர்கான் கால்நடைகளை மட்டுமே கொன்றது.
options: A is correct, R is incorrect || A is correct, R is incorrect
Both A and R are correct, but R is not the correct explanation of A || Both A and R are correct, but R is not the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct, and R is the correct explanation of A || Both A and R are correct, and R is the correct explanation of A
A is incorrect, R is correct || A is incorrect, R is correct
questions.ex: Because Shere Khan was lame, he killed cattle, which made the villagers of Waingunga angry, forcing him to shift his hunting grounds. || Because Shere Khan was lame, he killed cattle, which made the villagers of Waingunga angry, forcing him to shift his hunting grounds.

questions.q:
Match the character with their trait:
1. Tabaqui
2. Shere Khan
3. Mother Wolf || கதாபாத்திரத்தை அவர்களின் பண்புகளுடன் பொருத்துக: 1. தபாகி 2. ஷேர்கான் 3. தாய் ஓநாய்
options: 1-begs for meat, 2-lame foot, 3-grey nose || 1-begs for meat, 2-lame foot, 3-grey nose
1-grey nose, 2-begs for meat, 3-lame foot || 1-grey nose, 2-begs for meat, 3-lame foot
1-lame foot, 2-grey nose, 3-begs for meat || 1-lame foot, 2-grey nose, 3-begs for meat
1-begs for meat, 2-grey nose, 3-lame foot || 1-begs for meat, 2-grey nose, 3-lame foot
questions.ex: Tabaqui begs for meat; Shere Khan has a lame foot; Mother Wolf has a grey nose. || Tabaqui begs for meat; Shere Khan has a lame foot; Mother Wolf has a grey nose.

questions.q: Identify the type of sentence: 'How many kilos do you want?' || வாக்கியத்தின் வகையை அடையாளம் காணவும்: 'உங்களுக்கு எத்தனை கிலோ வேண்டும்?'
options: Declarative || Declarative
Imperative || Imperative
Exclamatory || Exclamatory
Interrogative || Interrogative
questions.ex: It is a question asking for information, so it is an Interrogative sentence. || It is a question asking for information, so it is an Interrogative sentence.

questions.q: If a tourist is visiting the Taj Mahal and says 'Wow! What a beautiful monument!', what kind of sentence is it? || தாஜ்மஹாலுக்கு வரும் ஒரு சுற்றுலாப் பயணி 'வாவ்! எவ்வளவு அழகான நினைவுச்சின்னம்!' என்று கூறினால், அது எவ்வகை வாக்கியம்?
options: Declarative || Declarative
Interrogative || Interrogative
Exclamatory || Exclamatory
Imperative || Imperative
questions.ex: Sentences expressing sudden or strong emotions, often ending with an exclamation mark, are Exclamatory sentences. || Sentences expressing sudden or strong emotions, often ending with an exclamation mark, are Exclamatory sentences.

questions.q: Fill in the correct article: 'Vishal drew ____ wonderful picture on the board yesterday.' || சரியான Article-ஐ நிரப்புக: 'விஷால் நேற்று போர்டில் ____ அற்புதமான படத்துவரைந்தார்.'
options: an || an
a || a
the || the
no article needed || no article needed
questions.ex: 'Wonderful' starts with a consonant sound, so the indefinite article 'a' is used. || 'Wonderful' starts with a consonant sound, so the indefinite article 'a' is used.

questions.q: Fill in the correct article: 'Sujatha is reading ____ interesting story in the library.' || சரியான Article-ஐ நிரப்புக: 'சுஜாதா நூலகத்தில் ____ சுவாரஸ்யமான கதையைப் படித்துக்கொண்டிருக்கிறாள்.'
options: a || a
the || the
an || an
no article || no article
questions.ex: 'Interesting' begins with a vowel sound, so the article 'an' is appropriate. || 'Interesting' begins with a vowel sound, so the article 'an' is appropriate.

questions.q: Choose the right adjective to complete the sentence: 'As I am tall, I have given my name for ____ jump.' || வாக்கியத்தை நிறைவு செய்யச் சரியான பெயரடையைத் (Adjective) தேர்ந்தெடுக்கவும்: 'நான் உயரமாக இருப்பதால், நான் ____ தாண்டுதலுக்கு என் பெயரைக் கொடுத்துள்ளேன்.'
options: long || long
high || high
triple || triple
short || short
questions.ex: Being tall is advantageous for the 'high' jump, which is the context of the sports day dialogue. || Being tall is advantageous for the 'high' jump, which is the context of the sports day dialogue.

questions.q: Choose the right adjective: 'I have enrolled myself only in a ____ events.' || சரியான பெயரடையைத் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கவும்: 'நான் ஒரு ____ நிகழ்வுகளில் மட்டுமே என்னைச் சேர்த்துள்ளேன்.'
options: few || few
much || much
little || little
all || all
questions.ex: 'Events' is a countable noun, so 'few' is the grammatically correct adjective to use here. || 'Events' is a countable noun, so 'few' is the grammatically correct adjective to use here.

questions.q: Fill in the blank with the correct article: 'Purvaja ate ____ salad of raw vegetables for dinner.' || சரியான Article-ஐ கொண்டு நிரப்புக: 'பூர்வஜா இரவு உணவிற்கு காய்கறிகளால் செய்யப்பட்ட ____ சாலட்டைச் சாப்பிட்டார்.'
options: an || an
the || the
a || a
some || some
questions.ex: The word 'salad' begins with a consonant sound, requiring the article 'a'. || The word 'salad' begins with a consonant sound, requiring the article 'a'.

questions.q: How did Father Wolf describe Tabaqui when he entered the cave? || தபாகி குகைக்குள் நுழைந்தபோது தந்தை ஓநாய் அவனை எவ்வாறு விவரித்தது?
options: A noble friend || A noble friend
A strong hunter || A strong hunter
So mean a person || So mean a person
A brave warrior || A brave warrior
questions.ex: Tabaqui himself says, 'for so mean a person as myself a dry bone is a good feast,' matching the wolves' low opinion of him. || Tabaqui himself says, 'for so mean a person as myself a dry bone is a good feast,' matching the wolves' low opinion of him.

questions.q: Why did Mother Wolf say she would keep the Man's cub? || மனிதக் குட்டியைத் தன்னுடன் வைத்துக்கொள்வதாக தாய் ஓநாய் ஏன் கூறியது?
options: Because he brought them food || Because he brought them food
Because he came naked, by night, alone, hungry, and yet was not afraid || Because he came naked, by night, alone, hungry, and yet was not afraid
Because Shere Khan asked her to keep him || Because Shere Khan asked her to keep him
Because Tabaqui gave him to her || Because Tabaqui gave him to her
questions.ex: She was impressed by his bravery. He was naked, hungry, and alone, yet he looked up and was completely unafraid. || She was impressed by his bravery. He was naked, hungry, and alone, yet he looked up and was completely unafraid.

questions.q: What does the purr of a hunting tiger do to woodcutters and gypsies? || வேட்டையாடும் புலியின் உறுமல் மரம் வெட்டுபவர்களையும் நாடோடிகளையும் என்ன செய்யும்?
options: It puts them to sleep || It puts them to sleep
It bewilders them and makes them run sometimes into the tiger's mouth || It bewilders them and makes them run sometimes into the tiger's mouth
It makes them angry || It makes them angry
It helps them find their way || It helps them find their way
questions.ex: The text notes that the humming purr from every compass quarter bewilders woodcutters and gypsies sleeping in the open. || The text notes that the humming purr from every compass quarter bewilders woodcutters and gypsies sleeping in the open.

questions.q:
Assertion (A): Mother Wolf was nicknamed 'The Demon'.
Reason (R): She won her place in a fair fight from five other wolves. || கூற்று (A): தாய் ஓநாய்க்கு அரக்கி என்று செல்லப்பெயர் சூட்டப்பட்டது. காரணம் (R): அவள் மற்ற ஐந்து ஓநாய்களிடம் நியாயமாகச் சண்டையிட்டு தனது இடத்தைப் பிடித்தாள்.
options: A is correct, R is wrong || A is correct, R is wrong
A is wrong, R is correct || A is wrong, R is correct
Both A and R are correct, and R explains A || Both A and R are correct, and R explains A
Both A and R are correct, but R does not explain A || Both A and R are correct, but R does not explain A
questions.ex: She was called Raksha (The Demon) for compliment's sake because she was a fierce fighter who won her place in the Pack. || She was called Raksha (The Demon) for compliment's sake because she was a fierce fighter who won her place in the Pack.

questions.q: What is the meaning of the word 'quarry' in the context of Shere Khan's dialogue? || ஷேர்கானின் உரையாடலின் சூழலில் 'quarry' என்ற வார்த்தையின் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: A stone mine || A stone mine
A deep hole || A deep hole
Prey or target of a hunt || Prey or target of a hunt
A friend || A friend
questions.ex: Shere Khan refers to the Man's cub as 'My quarry,' meaning his hunted prey. || Shere Khan refers to the Man's cub as 'My quarry,' meaning his hunted prey.

questions.q: What animals does Mowgli eventually befriend later in the story (as mentioned in the summary)? || (சுருக்கத்தில் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளபடி) கதையின் பிற்பகுதியில் மௌக்லி இறுதியில் எந்த விலங்குகளுடன் நட்பு கொள்கிறான்?
options: Bagheera and Baloo || Bagheera and Baloo
Elephants and Horses || Elephants and Horses
Tabaqui and Shere Khan || Tabaqui and Shere Khan
Only the Monkey gang || Only the Monkey gang
questions.ex: The summary mentions his friendship with Bagheera (the panther) and his adventurous trips with Baloo (the bear). || The summary mentions his friendship with Bagheera (the panther) and his adventurous trips with Baloo (the bear).

questions.q: At what time of day does Father Wolf wake up? || பகல் நேரத்தில் தந்தை ஓநாய் எந்த நேரத்தில் கண் விழிக்கிறது?
options: Early morning || Early morning
Noon || Noon
Seven o'clock in the evening || Seven o'clock in the evening
Midnight || Midnight
questions.ex: The scene opens at seven o'clock, a sunny evening, when Father Wolf wakes up from his day's rest. || The scene opens at seven o'clock, a sunny evening, when Father Wolf wakes up from his day's rest.

questions.q: What physical action did Father Wolf do to make himself ready for the hunt? || வேட்டைக்குத் தயாராக தந்தை ஓநாய் என்ன உடல் ரீதியான செயலைச் செய்தது?
options: He howled at the moon || He howled at the moon
He sharpened his claws on a tree || He sharpened his claws on a tree
He released and spread out his paws one after the other || He released and spread out his paws one after the other
He took a bath in the river || He took a bath in the river
questions.ex: He wakes up, releases, and spreads out his paws one after the other to get ready for the hunt. || He wakes up, releases, and spreads out his paws one after the other to get ready for the hunt.

questions.q: What did Mother Wolf do with her four tumbling cubs? || தாய் ஓநாய் தனது துள்ளிக்குதிக்கும் நான்கு குட்டிகளுடன் என்ன செய்தது?
options: She fed them to Shere Khan || She fed them to Shere Khan
She dropped them into the mouth of the cave where they all live || She dropped them into the mouth of the cave where they all live
She chased them into the river || She chased them into the river
She left them in the bushes || She left them in the bushes
questions.ex: Mother Wolf, with her big grey nose, dropped her four tumbling, squealing cubs into the mouth of the cave. || Mother Wolf, with her big grey nose, dropped her four tumbling, squealing cubs into the mouth of the cave.

questions.q: Fill in the blank with an article: 'Srinath and Sandhya were bored at ____ awful concert.' || ஒரு Article-ஐ கொண்டு நிரப்புக: 'ஸ்ரீநாத் மற்றும் சந்தியா ____ மோசமான கச்சேரியால் சலிப்படைந்தனர்.'
options: a || a
the || the
an || an
no article || no article
questions.ex: The word 'awful' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is the correct article. || The word 'awful' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is the correct article.

questions.q: According to the story, who takes orders from the Head of the Pack? || கதையின்படி, கூட்டத்தின் தலைவரிடமிருந்து யார் உத்தரவுகளைப் பெறுகிறார்கள்?
options: The Tigers || The Tigers
The Wolves || The Wolves
The Villagers || The Villagers
The Jackals || The Jackals
questions.ex: Father Wolf tells Shere Khan that 'The Wolves are a free people. They take orders from the Head of the Pack.' || Father Wolf tells Shere Khan that 'The Wolves are a free people. They take orders from the Head of the Pack.'

questions.q: How did Mother Wolf's eyes look when facing Shere Khan? || ஷேர்கானை எதிர்கொண்டபோது தாய் ஓநாயின் கண்கள் எப்படி இருந்தன?
options: Like two burning coals || Like two burning coals
Like two green moons in the darkness || Like two green moons in the darkness
Like bright stars || Like bright stars
Like yellow suns || Like yellow suns
questions.ex: The text beautifully describes her eyes as 'like two green moons in the darkness, facing the blazing eyes of Shere Khan.' || The text beautifully describes her eyes as 'like two green moons in the darkness, facing the blazing eyes of Shere Khan.'

questions.q: Identify the type of sentence: 'Please give me your pen.' || வாக்கியத்தின் வகையை அடையாளம் காணவும்: 'தயவுசெய்து உங்கள் பேனாவை எனக்குக் கொடுங்கள்.'
options: Declarative || Declarative
Interrogative || Interrogative
Imperative || Imperative
Exclamatory || Exclamatory
questions.ex: It is a request, which falls under the category of an Imperative sentence. || It is a request, which falls under the category of an Imperative sentence.
